title: Behavioral Eval Case
description: Spec for a single behavioral eval: one user prompt, expected events, and optional behavior text checks.

eval_id.description: Stable identifier; used as the primary key in ds_eval_baselines.

version.description: Semver. Bump when expected_events or expected_behavior changes meaningfully.

description.description: One sentence: what behavior is being tested.

skill_id.description: Optional: if set, ds eval run --skill filters to this skill's evals.

input_prompt.description: The user prompt injected into the session.

fixture_events.description: Pre-seeded event list for deterministic unit tests. Null means live runner captures real events.

expected_events.description: Events that must (or must not) appear in the session event log.

max_sequence_position.description: If set, event must appear at or before this 0-based position.

expected_behavior.description: 1-2 sentence description of the expected behavioral outcome. Graded by Opus judge (30% weight). Keep short — LLM-judge reliability degrades with length.

negative_checks.description: Behaviors that must NOT appear. Each string is a plain-language assertion checked by the judge.

event_weight.description: Fraction of composite score from deterministic event matching.

behavior_weight.description: Fraction of composite score from Opus judge behavior grading.

minimum_score.description: Composite score threshold; below this the eval is counted as failed.
